name: Paracetamol
warnings: Do not exceed recommended dose
Avoid with alcohol
Check other medications for paracetamol content
Liver disease — use with caution
dosage_note: Follow packaging instructions. Never exceed the maximum daily dose. Consult a pharmacist or doctor for personalised advice.

name: Ibuprofen
warnings: Take with food to reduce stomach irritation
Avoid if kidney or stomach problems
Avoid during pregnancy (especially 3rd trimester)
May worsen asthma in some people
dosage_note: Take with food or milk. Do not use continuously for more than a few days without medical advice. Consult a doctor for the right dose for your age and condition.

name: Aspirin
warnings: Do not give to children under 16
Avoid with blood-thinning medications
Caution with stomach ulcers
Can cause allergic reactions in aspirin-sensitive people
dosage_note: Low-dose aspirin for heart protection is usually prescribed by a doctor. For pain/fever, consult a pharmacist. Never use in children or teenagers with viral illness.

name: Amoxicillin
warnings: Do not use for viral infections (colds, flu)
Complete the full course
Serious allergy risk in penicillin-allergic people
Can cause C. difficile colitis
dosage_note: Prescription required. Complete the full course even if you feel better. Never share antibiotics or use leftover prescriptions.

name: Metformin
warnings: Risk of lactic acidosis (rare but serious)
Stop before contrast dye procedures
Caution with kidney disease
May cause B12 deficiency with long-term use
dosage_note: Prescription only. Take with meals to reduce side effects. Regular kidney function monitoring is recommended. Do not stop without medical advice.

name: Lisinopril
warnings: Can cause severe allergic reaction (angioedema)
Avoid in pregnancy
Monitor kidney function and potassium
Do not use with potassium supplements without advice
dosage_note: Prescription only. Take as prescribed. Do not stop suddenly. Regular blood pressure monitoring is important.

name: Atorvastatin
warnings: Report unexplained muscle pain or weakness immediately
Avoid grapefruit juice
Regular liver function tests recommended
Avoid in pregnancy
dosage_note: Prescription only. Usually taken in the evening. Do not stop without medical advice as this increases cardiovascular risk.

name: Omeprazole
warnings: Long-term use may reduce magnesium and B12 levels
May mask symptoms of stomach cancer
Risk of C. difficile with prolonged use
Interaction with clopidogrel
dosage_note: Available over-the-counter in low doses for short-term heartburn. Higher doses require a prescription. Take 30 minutes before meals for best effect.

name: Salbutamol
warnings: Use reliever inhaler only when needed
If using reliever more than twice a week, see your doctor
Overuse can worsen asthma control
Always carry inhaler with you
dosage_note: Prescription required. Use exactly as prescribed or instructed. Shake well before use. Rinse mouth if using with spacer.

name: Cetirizine
warnings: May cause drowsiness — caution when driving
Avoid with alcohol
Caution with kidney disease
dosage_note: Available over-the-counter. Usually taken once daily. Non-drowsy for most people but some experience sedation.

name: Sertraline
warnings: Takes 2-4 weeks for full effect
Do not stop abruptly — taper gradually
Risk of serotonin syndrome with certain combinations
Increased suicidal thoughts possible in young people initially
Avoid with MAOIs
dosage_note: Prescription only. Take at the same time each day. Give it 4-6 weeks to assess effectiveness. Never stop suddenly without medical guidance.

name: Diclofenac
common_side_effects: Increased cardiovascular risk with long-term use
warnings: Increased heart attack and stroke risk with prolonged use
Avoid with stomach ulcers
Avoid in late pregnancy
Use lowest effective dose for shortest time
dosage_note: Available as gel (often OTC) or tablets (prescription). For topical gel, apply directly to the painful area. Do not use on broken skin.

name: Loratadine
warnings: Generally non-drowsy but caution with some individuals
Caution with severe liver problems
dosage_note: Available over-the-counter. Take once daily. One of the most commonly used non-sedating antihistamines. Can be taken with or without food.

name: Codeine
warnings: Risk of dependence and addiction
Do not use in children under 12
Avoid with breathing problems
May cause respiratory depression at high doses
Ultra-rapid metabolisers may be affected more severely
dosage_note: Prescription required for higher doses. Use for the shortest time possible. Never share with others. Do not drive or operate machinery when taking.

name: Metoprolol
warnings: Do not stop suddenly — taper gradually
May mask hypoglycaemia in diabetics
Caution with asthma or COPD
May worsen peripheral artery disease
dosage_note: Prescription only. Take exactly as prescribed. Never stop abruptly without medical guidance as this can cause rebound heart problems.

name: Warfarin
warnings: Requires regular INR blood monitoring
Many drug and food interactions
Vitamin K (in leafy greens) affects levels
Serious bleeding risk
Tell all healthcare providers you are on warfarin
dosage_note: Prescription only. Dose adjusted based on regular blood tests (INR). Report any unusual bleeding immediately. Carry a warfarin alert card.

name: Prednisolone
warnings: Do not stop abruptly after long-term use
Increases infection susceptibility
Monitor blood glucose in diabetics
Long-term use causes osteoporosis risk
dosage_note: Prescription only. Take with food in the morning to reduce insomnia. Do not stop suddenly if on long-term treatment — the dose must be tapered.

name: Fluoxetine
warnings: Takes several weeks for full effect
Do not stop abruptly
Avoid with MAOIs (at least 14 days apart)
Monitor for suicidal thoughts in young people
Has a long half-life — effects persist after stopping
dosage_note: Prescription only. Full antidepressant effect takes 4-6 weeks. Do not stop suddenly. Tell your doctor about all other medications.

name: Amlodipine
warnings: Ankle oedema is common — report if severe
Grapefruit juice may increase drug levels
May cause low blood pressure initially
dosage_note: Prescription only. Take at the same time each day. Ankle swelling is common and usually not serious, but report it to your doctor.

name: Lactulose
warnings: May take 48 hours to work
Not suitable for galactose intolerance
Ensure adequate fluid intake
dosage_note: Available over-the-counter for constipation. May take 1-2 days to work. Drink plenty of fluids. Long-term use should be supervised by a doctor.

name: Trimethoprim
warnings: Complete the full course
Can interact with methotrexate and other medications
Caution in kidney disease
May cause photosensitivity
dosage_note: Prescription required. Complete the full antibiotic course. Drink plenty of water during treatment.

name: Glyceryl trinitrate (GTN)
warnings: Do not use with erectile dysfunction medications (sildenafil etc.) — dangerous drop in BP
May cause fainting — sit or lie when using
Tolerance develops with continuous use
Keep away from heat and light
dosage_note: Prescription only. Spray or tablet under the tongue for acute angina. If chest pain does not improve after 2 doses, call emergency services.

name: Chlorphenamine
warnings: Causes significant drowsiness — do not drive
Avoid with alcohol
Avoid in glaucoma
Caution in elderly due to confusion risk
dosage_note: Available over-the-counter. Often used for nighttime allergy relief due to sedating effect. Causes significant drowsiness — plan accordingly.

name: Bisoprolol
warnings: Never stop suddenly — risk of rebound heart problems
Caution in asthma
May mask hypoglycaemia signs in diabetics
dosage_note: Prescription only. Usually taken once daily in the morning. Do not stop without medical advice.

name: Doxycycline
warnings: Take upright with plenty of water — do not lie down for 30 minutes
Use sun protection
Avoid dairy and antacids close to dosing time
Not for children under 8 or during pregnancy
dosage_note: Prescription required. Take with a full glass of water, sitting upright. Avoid prolonged sun exposure. Complete the full course.
